items.title: Space and Lunar Interferometry: Emerging Concepts and Pathways
items.summary: arXiv:2607.07746v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Space- and lunar-based interferometry are moving from aspiration toward a concrete landscape of mission studies, pathfinders, and staged architectures. This invited review surveys that landscape across two principal platform pathway…

items.title: Multi-Wavelength Signatures of a Giant Cometary Radio Halo in MACSJ0417-1154
items.summary: arXiv:2607.07750v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Galaxy clusters hosting diffuse non-thermal radio emission offer direct insight into plasma processes of the intracluster medium (ICM). We present the first multi-frequency study of the radio halo in MACSJ0417 (z = 0.445) using uGMR…

items.summary: arXiv:2607.07765v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the results of a search for continuous gravitational waves from the low-mass X-ray binary Scorpius X-1 using LIGO data from the first part of the fourth LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA observing run. By applying the resampling version o…

items.title: The Status of Single Scalar Field Dark Energy
items.summary: arXiv:2607.07777v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present an assessment of the current observational status of single scalar field models of dark energy. Motivated by recent cosmological measurements -- including baryon acoustic oscillations, Type Ia supernovae, and CMB data --…

items.title: Stellar J-Harvesting: a novel angular momentum technosignature and first search in the Kepler field
items.summary: arXiv:2607.07781v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Stellar J-Harvesting (SJH), a technosignature in which an engineered system extracts rotational angular momentum from a star. Unlike a classical Dyson sphere, such a system need not produce a detectable mid-infrared exc…
